Mobile Money Transaction Levy [No. 16 of 2023 279
GOVERNMENT OF ZAMBIA
ACT
No. 16 of 2023
Date of Assent: 22nd December, 2023
An Act to provide for imposition, payment and collection of
a mobile money transaction levy and provide for matters
connected with, and incidental to, the foregoing.
[26th December, 2023
ENACTED by the Parliament of Zambia. Enactment
Short title

sections.heading: This Act may be cited as the Mobile Money Transaction
sections.text:
and
Levy Act, 2023, and shall come into operation on 1st January, 2024. commence-
ment

sections.heading: In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires— Interpretation
sections.text:
“Bank” means the Bank of Zambia established under the
Constitution; Cap.1
“betting” has the meaning assigned to the word in the Casino Cap. 157
Act;
“Consolidated Fund” means the Consolidated Fund established
under the Constitution; Cap. 1
“electronic money” means the store of monetary value
electronically represented by a claim on the mobile money
service provider that is—
(a) issued on receipt of funds in an amount not less in
value than the value of the electronic money issued;
(b) stored on an electronic device;
(c) accepted as a means of payment by persons; and
(d) redeemable on demand;;
280 No. 16 of 2023] Mobile Money Transaction Levy
“levy” means a mobile money transaction levy charged under
section 4;
“merchant” means a person or entity that accepts electronic
money as a means of payment for goods and services, but
does not include betting, and is registered with the Zambia
Revenue Authority for tax purposes;
“mobile money service provider” means an entity that is
authorised by the Bank to issue electronic money against
Act No. 1 of receipt of funds in accordance with the National Payment
2007 Systems Act, 2007;
“person-to-person transfer” means a transfer of electronic
money from one person to another person; and
“Zambia Revenue Authority” means the Zambia Revenue
Cap. 321 Authority established under the Zambia Revenue Authority
Act.
Administration 3. This Act shall be administered by the Bank.
of Act
Imposition of 4. (1) Subject to the provisions of this Act, a mobile money
levy service provider shall collect a levy set out in the Schedule on a
person-to-person transfer.
(2) The levy referred to under subsection (1) shall be paid by
the sender of the electronic money.
(3) The mobile money service provider shall, within ten days
after the end of each month, remit the total levies collected for that
month to the Bank account specified under subsection (5).
(4) A mobile money service provider shall, at the time of
remitting a levy under subsection (3), submit a return to the Bank in
the prescribed manner and form.
(5) The Bank shall, subject to the approval of the Ministry
responsible for finance, open and operate a special bank account at
the Bank for the purposes of receiving a levy from a mobile money
service provider.
(6) The Bank shall, on receipt of the levy under subsection
(3), deposit that levy in the Consolidated Fund.
(7) A mobile money service provider that contravenes
subsections (3) and (4) commits an offence and is liable, on
conviction, to a fine not exceeding two hundred thousand penalty
units or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years or, to
both.
Mobile Money Transaction Levy [No. 16 of 2023 281

(c ) transfer from a bank account.
(2) Despite subsection (1), the Minister may, by statutory
instrument, exempt a person from payment of a levy.

sections.heading: 1) The Bank shall maintain a mechanism of monitoring Monitoring
sections.text:
person-to-person transfers to which the levy relates.
(2) Despite the generality of subsection (1) the Bank may, for
purposes of monitoring—
(a) verify the returns submitted in accordance with section
4(4);
(b) access the mobile money service provider’s operations,
bank account and data relating to a levy to ensure
compliance with the Act; and
(c) require the mobile money service provider to submit any
other information that the Bank may consider necessary.

sections.heading: 1) The Bank may issue guidelines or directives as the Bank Guidelines
sections.text:
may consider necessary for the administration or better carrying or directives
out of this Act.
(2) The Bank shall publish the guidelines or directives issued
under subsection (1) in the Gazette.

sections.heading: The Minister may, by statutory instrument, make Regulations
sections.text:
Regulations for the better carrying out of the provisions of this Act.
